description_long: This is a military-style helicopter with an elongated, olive-green fuselage and a prominent main rotor mounted on top. The cockpit is located at the front, featuring angular, dark-tinted windows that wrap around the nose for enhanced visibility. The helicopter has a tail boom extending to the rear, ending in a vertical stabilizer with a small tail rotor on the right side. The landing gear consists of two parallel skids running beneath the fuselage, connected by crossbars for stability. Markings, including a white number '15' and a yellow insignia, are visible on the side, adding to its utilitarian appearance. The overall design suggests a robust, functional aircraft suitable for urban or military operations.
description: Olive-green military helicopter with angular cockpit windows, main and tail rotors, and sturdy landing skids. Markings and insignia are visible on the fuselage.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the helicopter, cockpit windows, main rotor, tail boom, tail rotor, landing skids, and visible markings.
description_view_1: Narrow front or rear view, primarily showing the thin profile of the helicopter and the main rotor blade edge-on.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with cockpit windows, main rotor, tail boom, tail rotor, landing skids, and different visible markings.
description_view_3: Narrow front or rear view, similar to view 1, showing the thin profile and main rotor blade edge-on.
attribute_rotor_blades: Two long, straight main rotor blades, likely made of composite or metal, colored black with no visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: Tail rotor is present on the right side of the tail boom, appears to have two blades, oriented vertically, colored black with no visible markings.
attribute_landing_skids: Two parallel, tubular landing skids made of metal, colored black, with visible crossbars connecting them for structural support.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Four angular cockpit windows, dark-tinted, wrapping around the front and sides of the cockpit, framed in black.
